title: Open Historiography
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"9ps76","text":"Open Historiography is the rule of law (RoL) academic
scholar application to historical studies of meta-historical
self-disclosure audit using the open access scholarship, open source
technologies, open science transparency best practices. It was
developed through years of open justice open diplomacy policymaking
along cultural projects social solidarity economy (SSE) development
with the most respected international organizations and was compiled
for the University of São Paulo (USP) Institute of Advanced Studies
(IEA) Urban Religion Summit to base the collaborative development of
my study on the historicity of open education.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"f5s87","text":"Follow each step precisely under local and international rule of law (RoL).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
